results.title: Pros and cons of Mira, Inito, Kegg, and other femtech devices - Natural Womanhood
results.content:
### Inito pros

Inito is valuable for those struggling with infertility or other cycle issues because of its ability to identify anovulatory cycles, which are the number one cause of infertility. Reviews mention the benefits of using Inito with PCOS and irregular cycles, and how grateful users were for the extra information they were able to garner about their cycles. It is also cheaper than Mira, coming to $149 for a starter kit and $49 for a refill of test strips (users with an average cycle length use 12-15 strips, according to the website, so that would come out to $49 a month for continued use of Inito). Inito, like Mira, is also HSA eligible.

### Inito cons [...] ## Inito

Next up on our review of femtech devices: Inito. Similar to Mira, Inito is an at-home hormone testing kit designed to be comprehensive and user-friendly. It also tracks four key hormones: estrogen, LH, PdG, and FSH. With its test-strip reading attachment for the iPhone, Inito’s results display in charts on your phone in the Inito app, which can be easily shared with medical providers. [...] Similar to ClearBlue, Inito gives qualitative results, such as “High Fertility,” “Peak Fertility,” or “Ovulation Confirmed,” but, like Mira, Inito also shows the user quantitative values of all four hormones. Uniquely, Inito requires only one test stick to test for all four hormones, rather than four separate ones. The one-strip-for-all-hormones feature may be especially useful for those with irregular or even anovulatory cycles, for whom two hormonal confirmations of ovulation (estrogen and progesterone) may be better than one.

### Inito pros

results.title: Inito vs Mira: I Tried Both Monitors - Which Is Best? - The PCOS Nutritionist Alyssa
results.content:
Unlike Mira, Inito does have the added benefit of testing for 4 hormones on a single Fertility Strip, since it also tests for FSH. You do have the option to test FSH using the Mira Hormone Monitor, however, you’d need to purchase the FSH strips for an additional cost. Although, if fertility is your goal, I don’t really think this is a huge factor since LH, E3G, and PdG give you the info you really want.

Inito’s new InSight Wireless Reader has made it so you no longer need to rely on your iPhone and its’ camera to get your results. You can just insert your urine Fertility Strip into the wireless reader and walk away. Inito also works seamlessly for both iPhone and Android users now. [...] I find the Inito charts to be easier to read and more aesthetically pleasing than the Mira charts. Inito also will put a color-coded vertical line on your chart on the days where your LH surged, ovulation occurred, and PdG increased to confirm ovulation.

The Inito app also “reads your charts” and provides you with daily directions as to whether you’ll need to take a hormone test that day or not. The app will also tell you when you’re in your fertile window and when you’ve actually ovulated.

## My Inito Charts [...] ## Cost of Inito Fertility Monitor

The start-up cost of the Inito is quite a bit lower than Mira. The Inito Starter Kit will cost you $169 USD, which comes with 15 Fertility Strips to last you about a month. You can take advantage of special launch pricing though which brings the InSight Wireless Reader Starter Kit to just $99 as part of the launch offer using my code: ALYSSAINITO

When you need to refill your test strip supply, it will cost you $49 USD for a 15-pack. Remember, you can expect to use approximately 12 to 15 test strips per month. You can purchase multiple packs of test strips at one time for a discounted rate…essentially, the more you buy, the lower each individual pack will cost.

results.title: Proov vs Mira Fertility Tests: Differences, Similarities & How to Choose Which is Best for You - Proov
results.content:
Additionally, the Mira Clarity Bundle requires the purchase of the Mira Analyzer to read the tests, while the free Proov Insight app allows you to read your Complete test results right on your phone. 

## How to Choose: Pros & Cons of Each

Since Proov Complete provides more extensive information about your cycle and fertility, and costs less on a per cycle basis, it provides more value for the price. Additionally, you don’t need to purchase an external test reader to interpret the results since the free Proov Insight app reads, interprets, and saves your results across your cycle.

However, if you’re specifically interested in finding your fertile window, Mira could be a good option for you. [...] ## Similarities & Differences Between the Two

Proov Complete and the Mira Clarity Bundle both apparently measure similar hormone markers (FSH, LH, and PdG), with the only difference being Complete measures E1G and Mira measures E3G.

The Mira Clarity Bundle purports to predict and confirm ovulation via LH, E3G, and PdG to find the fertility window. It also measures FSH to tell how far you are from menopause and to assess your fertility health. 

Proov Complete, on the other hand, focuses on providing powerful insight throughout your cycle – not just during the fertile window. Complete measures hormones during 3 key testing windows during your cycle to provide insight into your ovarian reserve, full fertile window, and successful ovulation. [...] The Mira Clarity Bundle contains the following:

 1 Mira Analyzer (device that reads the testing wands)
 Mira carrying pouch
 Charging cable
 10 Single-use Estradiol Metabolite (E3G) + Luteinizing Hormone (LH) + Pregnanediol-3-Glucuronide (PdG) Test Wands (Mira Max Wands) – may not be enough for 1 cycle
 10 Single-use Follicle Stimulating Hormone (FSH) Test Wands (Mira Ovum Wands)
 1 Silicone Cup
 Quickstart Guide: Step-by-step instructions to get started with Mira
 Mira app

### How does it work?

With the Mira Clarity Bundle, you should expect to test 10-20 times per cycle, depending on your body’s unique rhythm (note that because there are only 10 wands of each kind, you may run out). Each time you test, you’ll want to dip the wand in your urine sample for 10-20 seconds.

results.content:

# The Mira App

Mira is more than just a hormone tracker - it’s an all-in-one fertility tracking system that automatically syncs, records, and explains your fertility hormone data in a way that is easy to understand.

[](   [](

## More than a period and fertility tracking app

 

### Track your hormones

See your actual hormone concentrations and receive personalized fertility insights based on your unique hormone data.

### View hormone curve

The Mira App uses AI algorithms to learn your changing hormone patterns for a highly personalized and accurate ovulation prediction specific to you.

### Advanced calendar

The Mira App lets you record specific health details about your cycles so you can predict and view your fertile window more precisely.
